Race Ipsa Loquitur

"The timorous may stay at home." — Murphy v. Steeplechase Amusement Co., 250 N.Y. 479 (1929)

Home Archive About

About

Amelia Boone is a 4x World Champion obstacle course racer, ultramarathon runner, and attorney.

She won World's Toughest Mudder in 2012 and 2014, and placed 2nd overall (beating all but one man) in 2013. She is a 3x Spartan Race World Champion and has completed some of the most grueling endurance events in the world, including the Barkley Marathons and multiple 100-mile ultramarathons.

By day, Amelia works as corporate counsel at Apple. She graduated from Washington University in St. Louis and the University of Virginia School of Law.

Her writing explores the intersection of endurance sport, injury, recovery, and mental health. She has been open about her experiences with eating disorder recovery and the psychological challenges of being a competitive athlete.

This archive contains selected posts from her blog "Race Ipsa Loquitur" (2011-2023). For current writing, visit her Substack.

Now writing at Substack
